name: context traffic light

meta.description: A precise context window monitor (context traffic light) that reads the hardware limit directly from the Open WebUI SQLite database and displays the exact token usage from the Ollama metadata. This is visualized using traffic light colors. Token values ​​are also output. Note: This function must be selected in the model used after creation to be displayed in the chat. It becomes active after the first input in the chat. The output names can easily be adapted to the preferred language in the code; here it is in German. The traffic light percentage values ​​can also be changed as desired. - Important addendum: it only works if the default context window value "num_ctx" is changed!

manifest.title: KontextTraffic Light 15.0 - Final Precision
manifest.author: taldoc
manifest.description: Nutzt SQL für das Workspace-Limit und das Usage-Feld für exakte Token-Zahlen. Ohne Fallback-Zahlen.

content:
"""
title: KontextTraffic Light 15.0 - Final Precision
author: taldoc
version: 15.0
description: Nutzt SQL für das Workspace-Limit und das Usage-Feld für exakte Token-Zahlen. Ohne Fallback-Zahlen.
"""

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, Union
import sqlite3
import json
import os

class Filter:
    class Valves(BaseModel):
        db_path: str = Field(
            default="/app/backend/data/webui.db",
            description="Pfad zur Open WebUI Datenbank."
        )

    def __init__(self):
        self.valves = self.Valves()

    def get_true_limit(self, model_id: str) -> Optional[int]:
        """Holt das exakte num_ctx Limit aus der SQLite Datenbank. Gibt None bei Fehler zurück."""
        if os.path.exists(self.valves.db_path):
            try:
                conn = sqlite3.connect(self.valves.db_path)
                cursor = conn.cursor()
                cursor.execute("SELECT params FROM model WHERE id = ?", (model_id,))
                row = cursor.fetchone()
                conn.close()
                
                if row and row[0]:
                    params = json.loads(row[0])
                    # Wir suchen num_ctx in den Parametern
                    val = params.get("num_ctx") or params.get("options", {}).get("num_ctx")
                    if val:
                        return int(val)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Fehler beim Datenbank-Abruf: {e}")
        return None

    def inlet(self, body: dict, __user__: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict:
        return body

    def outlet(self, body: dict, __user__: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict:
        messages = body.get("messages", [])
        if not messages:
            return body

        # 1. DAS ECHTE LIMIT FINDEN
        model_id = body.get("model", "")
        max_c = self.get_true_limit(model_id)

        # 2. NUTZUNGSDATEN (Echte Tokens von Ollama)
        last_msg = messages[-1]
        usage = last_msg.get("usage", {})
        total_used = usage.get("total_tokens", 0)
        
        if total_used == 0:
            total_used = usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) + usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)

        # 3. AUSGABE GENERIEREN
        if total_used > 0:
            if max_c:
                # Normaler Modus mit Berechnung
                free = max_c - total_used
                percent = (total_used / max_c) * 100
                led = "🟢" if percent < 76 else "🟡" if percent < 97 else "🔴"
                limit_display = f"{max_c:,}"
                free_display = f"| **{max(0, free):,} frei**"
            else:
                # Fehler-Modus: Kein Limit gefunden
                led = "⚪"
                limit_display = "Limit prüfen!"
                free_display = ""

            status_line = (
                f"\n\n---\n"
                f"{led} **Kontextlimit:** {total_used:,} / {limit_display} benutzt {free_display}"
            )
            
            body["messages"][-1]["content"] += status_line

        return body
